runs.eval_name: Add participant and promote to admin

expectations.text: Agent selects addParticipants (not createGroup, not send, not invite)
participants array contains 972556839823@c.us (with @c.us suffix)
groupId is 120363421825201386@g.us (with @g.us suffix)
Agent also calls promoteToAdmin as a follow-up action
promoteToAdmin uses the same groupId and participant JID
expectations.evidence: eval-1.md: same groupId and participant JID in promoteToAdmin

expectations.text: Agent selects getInviteCode (not joinGroup, not getGroup, not createGroup)
groupId parameter is present and equals 120363421825201386@g.us
Agent does NOT call send or any messaging action
expectations.evidence: eval-2.md: 'NOT any messaging action — this is a query operation'

expectations.text: Agent selects blockContact (not unblockContact, not delete, not muteChat)
expectations.evidence: eval-3.md: 'Action: blockContact' with explicit rejection of alternatives

expectations.text: contactId parameter uses @c.us suffix: 972556839823@c.us
Agent does NOT call getContacts or any listing action first
expectations.evidence: eval-3.md: contactId already known from phone number, no listing needed

runs.eval_name: Get contact picture and about text

expectations.text: Agent calls getContactPicture with contactId 972556839823@c.us
Agent also calls getContactAbout with the same contactId
Both actions use @c.us suffix on the phone number
Agent does NOT conflate the two into a single action
expectations.evidence: eval-4.md: 'These are separate actions and must both be called'

expectations.text: Agent does NOT call followChannel with the raw URL or the invite code string as channelId
expectations.evidence: eval-5.md: Two explicit WRONG approach blocks showing correct rejection

expectations.text: Agent identifies the need to resolve the invite code to a newsletter JID first
expectations.evidence: eval-5.md: 'MUST NOT pass the URL or invite code directly to followChannel'

expectations.text: If agent uses a single action, it uses followChannel with a @newsletter JID (not the URL)
Agent explains or demonstrates the two-step process: resolve invite code → follow channel
expectations.evidence: eval-5.md: Explicit two-step process: searchChannelsByText → followChannel

expectations.text: Agent selects sendLocation (not send, not sendImage, not sendFile)
latitude parameter is 32.0853
longitude parameter is 34.7818
title parameter is 'Office' or equivalent
Agent selects deleteGroup (not leaveGroup, not delete, not archiveChat)
expectations.evidence: eval-7.md: 'Action: deleteGroup' with disambiguation table showing all wrong alternatives

expectations.text: groupId parameter is present and equals 120363421825201386@g.us
Agent explains the distinction: deleteGroup permanently deletes the group, leaveGroup only removes the bot
expectations.evidence: eval-7.md: 'Permanently removes the group from WhatsApp... leaveGroup — The bot exits the group but the group continues to exist'

expectations.text: Agent selects the read action (not readMessages, not getChatMessages)
expectations.evidence: eval-8.md: 'Action: read' selected with explicit rejection of readMessages and getChatMessages

expectations.text: chatId parameter is set to 120363421825201386@g.us
Agent does NOT confuse read (send receipts) with readMessages (fetch content)
expectations.evidence: eval-8.md: disambiguation table from messaging.md reproduced, explicit statement of distinction

notes: Single configuration run (with_skill only) — no without_skill baseline available
All 8 evals passed 100% of expectations (30/30 total)
Evals test decision-making (action selection + parameter construction) not live WAHA execution
Eval 5 (channel invite link) and Eval 7 (deleteGroup disambiguation) and Eval 8 (read vs readMessages) specifically test error-prone action selection
Category coverage: groups (evals 1,2,7), contacts (evals 3,4), channels (eval 5), messaging (evals 6,8)
